perturbation.original_text: "Provider" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.
perturbation.changed_text: "Vendor" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.
perturbation.explanation: The contract defines 'Provider' as the entity providing services, but it has been altered here to 'Vendor'. This introduces an in-text contradiction, as 'Provider' is used throughout the contract to define obligations, while 'Vendor' is not consistently referenced. This creates confusion about which entity is responsible for providing the services. The use of 'Vendor' where the text defines 'Provider' will cause confusion on the duties described.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.1 Quality of Services. (a) Provider shall perform the Services (i) at a level of quality substantially similar in all material respects to that at which such Services were performed or enjoyed during the twelve (12) month period prior to the date hereof and (ii) in accordance with applicable Law (collectively, (i) and (ii), the "Service Standard"). Subject to Section 3.1(c), internal controls of Provider and its Affiliates with respect to the Service Standard shall remain materially the same in effect throughout the term of this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the other Party and their Affiliates are not professional service providers of the Services. (b) In the event of any material failure of a Provider to perform the Services, as applicable, in accordance with the Service Standards, Recipient shall provide Provider with written notice of such material failure, and Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to remedy such failure as soon as reasonably possible and in the same manner that Provider would remedy such a failure for their other businesses undergoing such a material failure. (c) A Provider may, from time to time: (i) reasonably supplement, modify, upgrade, substitute or otherwise alter ("Change") any Service in a manner consistent with Changes made with respect to similar services provided by Provider on their own behalf or to their Affiliates, including taking any physical or information security measures with respect to such Service, in a manner that does not (x) adversely affect in any material respect the quality or availability of such Service or (y) materially increase the fees payable in connection with such Changed Service; provided that to the extent that any such Change is reasonably likely to modify, substitute or otherwise alter the receipt or use of such Service, Provider shall provide Recipient with reasonable advance written notice of the implementation of the Change to the extent practicable under the circumstances; provided, further, that the Service Standard shall continue to apply to such Service following any Change. If a Change is required by applicable Law or is in response to a threatened Security Incident, Provider may make any and all changes to the Service necessary to comply with applicable Law and any changes thereto or to respond to such threatened Security Incident in a manner consistent with responses made by Provider on its own behalf or in respect of their Affiliates; provided that Provider shall provide Recipient such reasonable advance written notice of the implementation of any such Change as may be practicable under the circumstances; and (ii) with reasonable advance written notice to Recipient, temporarily suspend the provision of a Service as necessary to conduct Systems maintenance or patching without such suspension constituting a breach of the Service Standard.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Provider' to 'Vendor' creates a strong in-text contradiction. Section 3.1 consistently refers to 'Provider' and their obligations regarding service quality. By redefining 'Provider' as 'Vendor' in the preamble definition, the contract introduces ambiguity and inconsistency as to which entity is responsible for fulfilling these obligations. This misaligned terminology directly contradicts the clear and consistent use of 'Provider' throughout Section 3.1, causing potential disputes and rendering the enforcement of service obligations uncertain. As the explanation states, 'Provider' is used throughout the contract to define obligations, while 'Vendor' is not consistently referenced.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall pay Provider (i) the fee for each Service set forth on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, (ii) Providers' and their Affiliates' reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing the Services, including the third-party fees and expenses that are charged to Recipient or their Affiliates in connection with provision of the Services (including any fees and expenses charged by subcontractors permitted to provide the Services under Section 2.2) but excluding payments made to employees of Provider or any of their Affiliates pursuant to Section 5.2, and (iii) any other fees as agreed to by the Parties in writing (collectively, the "Fees").
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall pay Contractor (i) the fee for each Service set forth on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, (ii) Contractors' and their Affiliates' reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing the Services, including the third-party fees and expenses that are charged to Recipient or their Affiliates in connection with provision of the Services (including any fees and expenses charged by subcontractors permitted to provide the Services under Section 2.2) but excluding payments made to employees of Provider or any of their Affiliates pursuant to Section 5.2, and (iii) any other fees as agreed to by the Parties in writing (collectively, the "Fees").
perturbation.explanation: The contract originally states that the 'Recipient shall pay Provider', the term 'Provider' was changed to 'Contractor'. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity in the payment obligations because throughout the contract, the one entitled to receive payment is known as the 'Provider' and by chaning the text with 'Contractor' makes the payment duties unclear on who is supossed to be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Provider shall submit or cause to be submitted to Recipient in writing, within 15 days after the end of each month, an invoice setting forth the Fees for the Services provided to Recipient during such month in reasonable detail, as applicable, due under such invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Provider' with 'Contractor' in a clause regarding payment obligations. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Provider' submitting invoices for services. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text implies that 'Contractor' is to be paid, while the contradicted text states that 'Provider' is to submit invoices to receive payment. Since the text uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, this constitutes a misaligned terminology that leads to an in-text contradiction, according to the definition provided, making the payment duties unclear on who is supossed to be paid.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall, and shall cause any of its Affiliates or third parties providing or receiving Services (as the case may be) to, follow the reasonable policies, procedures and practices of the other Party and its Affiliates applicable to the Services that are known or made known to such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall, and shall cause any of its Affiliates or third parties providing or receiving Deliverables (as the case may be) to, follow the reasonable policies, procedures and practices of the other Party and its Affiliates applicable to the Services that are known or made known to such Party.
perturbation.explanation: The contract states that each party must follow the policies and practices of the other party related to 'Services.' However, this has been altered to 'Deliverables'. This creates an in-text contradiction because 'Services' and 'Deliverables' are not defined as interchangeable, and 'Deliverables' is not otherwise referenced in the contract. This contradiction affects the scope of what policies and procedures each party is bound to follow, creating ambiguity and a dispute on the duties to be performed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other than as expressly set forth on Exhibit A, Section 2.1, Exhibit B, or as agreed by the Parties in writing, in no event shall Provider be obligated to provide any Service to the Recipient for any purpose other than to facilitate, on a transitional basis, the Recipient's ability to conduct business as conducted immediately preceding the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Services,' establishing policies for services provided or received. The changed text replaces 'Services' with 'Deliverables.' This change introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text, Exhibit A, Section 2.1, Exhibit B, refers to 'Service.' By changing the scope to 'Deliverables', the policy adherence requirement no longer aligns with the 'Service' mentioned in the contradicted text, creating ambiguity regarding which policies and procedures are applicable, and therefore a strong contradiction.
